case_title: Complaint experiencing delay and procedural narrowing during oversight process
decision_trigger: A complaint remained active across multiple stages but responses became narrower and more procedural over time.
recall_question: Do the case signals indicate delay, procedural containment, or escalation readiness?
desired_outcome: Establish whether the complaint remains meaningfully active or has shifted into procedural containment.
rules_or_procedures: External oversight escalation after delayed or inadequate response

actors.name: Citizen complainant
actors.role_in_case: raising, documenting, and following up the complaint
actors.incentive_or_pressure: seek procedural clarity and substantive engagement
actors.likely_behaviour: preserve chronology, monitor deadlines, and prepare escalation

actors.name: Public Body Complaints Unit
actors.role_in_case: initial complaint intake and first-stage responses
actors.incentive_or_pressure: manage complaint volume and reduce procedural exposure
actors.likely_behaviour: acknowledge complaint, narrow scope, and rely on procedure

actors.name: Internal Review Office
actors.role_in_case: secondary internal review of the complaint
actors.likely_behaviour: frame issues narrowly and defer substantive resolution

actors.name: External Oversight Body
actors.role_in_case: potential escalation route after internal delay
actors.incentive_or_pressure: review whether complaint falls within remit and is sufficiently evidenced
actors.likely_behaviour: seek chronology, records, and proof of internal exhaustion

evidence_bundle.label: Initial complaint submission
evidence_bundle.relevance: Establishes the complaint start point and issues raised

evidence_bundle.label: Acknowledgement email
evidence_bundle.relevance: Confirms receipt of complaint by the institution

evidence_bundle.label: Procedural clarification response
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows a narrow procedural response without substantive engagement

evidence_bundle.label: Follow-up request
evidence_bundle.relevance: Demonstrates continued effort to obtain substantive response

deadlines.label: Internal review follow-up deadline
deadlines.consequence: Failure to respond strengthens the case for external escalation

timeline.label: Initial complaint submitted
timeline.description: The complainant submitted the original complaint to the Public Body Complaints Unit.

timeline.label: Complaint acknowledged
timeline.description: The institution acknowledged receipt of the complaint.

timeline.label: Procedural response issued
timeline.description: The institution issued a procedural response that did not address the substance of the complaint.

timeline.label: Follow-up request submitted
timeline.description: The complainant requested substantive clarification and next procedural steps.

timeline.label: Internal review referenced
timeline.description: The institution referenced internal review pathways without resolving the issues raised.

escalation_paths.name: Internal review follow-up
escalation_paths.trigger: Substantive response remains absent after procedural correspondence
escalation_paths.likely_response: Further procedural narrowing or delayed clarification

escalation_paths.name: External oversight escalation
escalation_paths.trigger: Internal route appears delayed or procedurally exhausted
escalation_paths.likely_response: Request for complaint chronology, correspondence, and evidence bundle

linked_cases.note: Both cases involve procedural acknowledgement followed by delayed substantive movement.

structural_insight: The complaint appears to remain active procedurally while substantive engagement narrows over time.
personal_positioning: The complainant is maintaining a measured and evidence-based record while preparing for possible escalation.
decision_note: Continue documenting delays, preserve chronology, and prepare escalation if the next procedural window expires without meaningful engagement.
learning_capture: Repeated procedural responses without substantive movement can indicate emerging containment dynamics rather than active resolution.
